business_problem: We lose 40% of enterprise accounts after year 1. We need to flag at-risk accounts 90 days before renewal and deflect common support queries to reduce $8/ticket cost.
constraint_summary: CRM data, NPS scores, and support ticket history are available and sufficient for a churn prediction model. Product telemetry is not instrumented, ruling out usage-pattern anomaly detection. Email outreach via Mailchimp is available for win-back campaigns. No PII or regulatory blockers identified.

ml_problems.name: Churn Prediction
ml_problems.description: Predict which B2B accounts are at risk of churning within 90 days of renewal, using ARR, NPS score, support ticket volume, and contract age as predictors.
hypothesis_evidence: Accounts with NPS ≤ 2 churn at 84% vs 38% baseline (chi-sq p=0.0001) — strong signal
Accounts with >20 support tickets/month churn at 71% vs 38% (lift=1.87) — strong signal
business_kpis: Reduce logo churn by 15% over two quarters
Flag at-risk accounts 90 days before renewal to enable proactive CSM outreach

dataset.description: Historical B2B SaaS account records with binary churn label. Each row is one account measurement period.

target_column.reason: Binary churn outcome — direct target for binary_classification task

ml_problems.name: Support Query Deflection
ml_problems.description: Build a RAG pipeline that answers common support queries from a knowledge base, deflecting tickets that don't need human resolution.
hypothesis_evidence: Help centre corpus is available (850 articles) — RAG is directly feasible
business_kpis: Deflect 30% of inbound support tickets to automated RAG answers

dataset.description: AutoRAG requires two files: (1) corpus.csv — knowledge base documents (doc_id, contents); (2) qa_eval.csv — ground-truth QA pairs (qid, query, retrieval_gt, generation_gt).
